channel.title: Pima County Defender Practice Notes
channel.description: Short, plain-language notes on Arizona criminal procedure, the Sixth Amendment, and indigent defence from the desk of a working public defender. Not legal advice.

item.title: What I tell a 1L about Strickland before they read it
item.dc:creator: Randy Holt
item.description: The two-prong test is the easy part. The hard part is what counts as 'deficient' on a 35-file desk and why that ceiling matters every Monday morning.
item.content:encoded:
If you are reading Strickland for the first time, read the dissent twice. The majority gives you a test you can recite. The dissent tells you what the test buys, and what it does not.

The two prongs are deficient performance and prejudice. The trap is that both are measured against a hypothetical lawyer with a hypothetical caseload.

item.title: Crawford notes from a domestic-violence motion practice week
item.dc:creator: Randy Holt
item.description: Three motions in three days, all turning on whether the 911 audio is testimonial. Short field report and the cases I actually cited.
item.content:encoded: Two of the three motions turned on the timing of the 911 call versus the moment of the alleged ongoing emergency. The third was a clean primary-purpose argument and the audio came in over my objection. Notes below on each.

item.title: Why the first-Wednesday clinic matters more than my brief-writing day
item.dc:creator: Randy Holt
item.description: The first Wednesday of every month is the four hours that quietly do more than the rest of the month combined. A short explanation of why.
item.content:encoded: The Desert Bloom legal clinic is not glamorous. It is one woman at a folding table, a stack of forms, and a question she has been carrying for three months. I leave at 8:30 PM with handwriting cramp and a sense that something useful happened.

item.title: What 'effective assistance' looks like at 36 files
item.dc:creator: Randy Holt
item.description: A short, honest accounting of where the hours actually go when the desk is one file over the line.
item.content:encoded: I keep the count visible on my legal pad. Last quarter we crossed thirty-five. Triage starts here.

item.title: Trauma-informed cross, a working checklist
item.dc:creator: Randy Holt
item.description: Six questions I ask myself before I stand up to cross a witness with a trauma history. The work is harder. The cross is usually better.
item.content:encoded: This is what I share at CLEs. It is a checklist, not a doctrine. Adjust it to your case.

item.title: February reading: three books for new defenders
item.dc:creator: Randy Holt
item.description: Short reviews of Lewis on Gideon, Feige on the everyday, and Forman on the system. Pick one.
item.content:encoded: If you are taking your first felony desk this year, start with Lewis. Then Feige. Then Forman.

item.title: Notes from the State Bar of Arizona CLE, January 2026
item.dc:creator: Randy Holt
item.description: The five updates from the January programme that actually changed something on my desk.
item.content:encoded: Most of the programme was familiar. Five items were not.
